content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 30, 1992

Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Bell & Hiltachk
555 Capitol Mall
Suite 530
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-150

Dear Mr. Bell:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of the Non-Partisan Candidate Evaluation Council under the
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1

QUESTION

If the Non-Partisan Candidate Evaluation Council, a slate
mailer organization as defined by the Act, receives, or expects to
receive, less than $100 from a candidate or other person to appear
on a slate mailer, is the organization required to place an
asterisk on the slate mailer next to the name of the candidate
from, or on behalf of whom, the payment is received?

CONCLUSION

An asterisk is not required to be placed next to the name of
any candidate from whom, or at whose behest, payments totaling
less than $100 have been received.

FACTS

The Non-Partisan Candidate Evaluation Council is a slate
mailer organization within the meaning of Section 82048.5. It
publishes a slate mailer targeted to some 3.5 million households

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Our File No. A-92-150
Page 2

of registered voters during each regular primary and general
election every two years. Occasionally, it also publishes a slate
mailer in connection with municipal or local elections during odd-
numbered years.

The Non-Partisan Candidate Evaluation Council selects some
candidates and ballot measures which are endorsed without charge.
It also solicits other candidates and measures which pay to appear
on the slate mailings. All such candidates and measures placed on
the slates are those whose positions and viewpoints are consistent
with the principles and views of the organization. It charges a
market price for such paying candidates and measures.
Occasionally, it will sell positions on the slate to candidates or
groups of candidates at the county central committee level.
Because county central committee offices are not considered to be
public offices, the office of the central committee member is an
unpaid position, and the candidates are volunteers. The Non-
Partisan Candidate Evaluation Council often prices a county
central committee position on the slate at less than $100.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires sender identification on political mailings
of 200 or more pieces by a candidate or a committee. (Section
84305.) In 1987, additional amendments were enacted to impose
further requirements where the mailing is made in support of four
or more candidates or ballot measures and is made by an entity
which is paid to produce the slate mailer. (Sections 82048.3,
82048.5, and 84305.5.)

Section 84305.5 requires that slate mailers contain certain
specified information, including the asterisk requirements which
are the subject of your inquiry. Section 84305.5 states in part:

* * *

(a)(4) Each candidate and each ballot measure which has
paid to appear in the slate mailer is designated by an *.
Any candidate or ballot measure which has not paid to appear
in the slate mailer is not designated by an *.

* * *

(b) For purposes of the designations required by
paragraph (4) of subdivision (a), the payment of any sum made
reportable by the provisions of subdivision (c) of Section
84219 by or at the behest of a candidate or committee, whose
name or position appears in the mailer, to the slate mailer
organization, shall constitute a payment to appear, requiring
the * designation. Such payment shall also be deemed to
constitute authorization to appear in the mailer.

Section 84219 specifies the information required to be
disclosed by slate mailer organizations on their periodic campaign

Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Our File No. A-92-150
Page 3

statements. Subdivision (c) requires itemization of candidates
and committees which have paid $100 or more during the period
covered by the campaign statement to appear on a slate mailer.
Therefore, the reference in Section 84305.5(b) to the payment of
any sum made reportable by Section 84219(c) indicates that the
asterisk requirement is not applicable to candidates and
committees from whom, or at whose behest, payments totaling less
than $100 have been received by the slate mailer organization.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.2

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature: Carla Wardlow]

By: Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

___________________________

2 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
